```{r filter-low-detection-genes}
    target_spatial_data[fData(target_spatial_data)$DetectionRate >= params$qc$min_gene_detection_rate_across_segments |fData(target_spatial_data)$TargetName %in% neg_probes, ]

features_after_gene_filter <- nrow(target_spatial_data)
features_dropped_gene_filter <- features_before_gene_filter - features_after_gene_filter
gene_drop_fraction <- if (features_before_gene_filter > 0) {
  features_dropped_gene_filter / features_before_gene_filter
} else {
  0
}

message(sprintf(
  "Gene detection-rate filter (>= %s of segments): kept %d of %d features (%s removed).",
  scales::percent(params$qc$min_gene_detection_rate_across_segments, accuracy = 1),
  features_after_gene_filter,
  features_before_gene_filter,
  scales::percent(gene_drop_fraction, accuracy = 1)
))

if (gene_drop_fraction >= 0.5) {
  warning(sprintf(
    paste0(
      "The gene detection-rate filter removed %s of features (%d of %d). ",
      "A near-total drop usually reflects low panel-wide signal or too few segments retained in an earlier step; ",
      "review segment QC and `qc$min_gene_detection_rate_across_segments` before continuing."
    ),
    scales::percent(gene_drop_fraction, accuracy = 1),
    features_dropped_gene_filter,
    features_before_gene_filter
  ), call. = FALSE)
}

#save the filtered dataset
if (isTRUE(params$save$save_rds)) {
  saveRDS(target_spatial_data, file = stage1_filtered_contract)
}
```

Before moving to the next notebook, check whether enough retained segments remain for the main comparison you intend to run later. Set `key_annotation_column` in the YAML to the study group you care about most, such as `disease_status`, `condition`, or `treatment_group`. The next audit counts retained segments after gene LOQ filtering by that group and by segment type. The review threshold is only a prompt to inspect group balance; it does not filter samples.

### Check Retained Segments for the Main Comparison

Use this plot and table to decide whether the retained dataset is still suitable for the planned comparison. Low counts in one annotation/segment group may mean the contrast is underpowered, even if the overall QC summary looks acceptable. The dashed line marks the YAML-configured review threshold for this run (`r params$key_annotation_min_segments_per_group` retained segments per annotation/segment group). Treat this threshold as a decision aid for reviewing group balance, not as an automatic filter.

```{r build-key-annotation-retained-segment-audit}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

scalar_param <- function(x, default = "") {
  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0) return(default)
  if (is.list(x) && "value" %in% names(x)) x <- x$value
  if (length(x) == 0) return(default)

  value <- trimws(as.character(x[[1]]))
  if (is.na(value) || !nzchar(value)) default else value
}

resolve_metadata_column <- function(configured_col, available_cols, label) {
  if (is.na(configured_col) || !nzchar(configured_col)) return(NA_character_)

  exact_match <- available_cols[available_cols == configured_col][1]
  if (!is.na(exact_match)) return(exact_match)

  configured_clean <- janitor::make_clean_names(configured_col, case = "snake")
  available_clean <- janitor::make_clean_names(available_cols, case = "snake")
  cleaned_match <- available_cols[available_clean == configured_clean][1]

  if (!is.na(cleaned_match)) {
    warning(
      "Configured ", label, " column '", configured_col,
      "' was matched after column-name cleaning as '", cleaned_match, "'.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
    return(cleaned_match)
  }

  warning(
    "Configured ", label, " column '", configured_col,
    "' was not found. Available metadata columns: ",
    paste(available_cols, collapse = ", "),
    call. = FALSE
  )
  NA_character_
}

key_annotation_column <- scalar_param(params$key_annotation_column, default = "")
key_annotation_min_segments <- suppressWarnings(
  as.numeric(scalar_param(params$key_annotation_min_segments_per_group, default = "3"))
)

if (is.na(key_annotation_min_segments) || key_annotation_min_segments < 1) {
  warning("key_annotation_min_segments_per_group must be a positive number. Using 3 for this audit.")
  key_annotation_min_segments <- 3
}

final_segment_metadata <- pData(target_spatial_data) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "geomx_sample_id")

segment_audit_col <- resolve_metadata_column(
  configured_col = "segment",
  available_cols = colnames(final_segment_metadata),
  label = "segment"
)

if (is.na(segment_audit_col)) {
  final_segment_metadata$.segment_group <- "Segment column unavailable"
  segment_audit_col <- ".segment_group"
}

key_annotation_col <- resolve_metadata_column(
  configured_col = key_annotation_column,
  available_cols = colnames(final_segment_metadata),
  label = "key annotation"
)

if (!nzchar(key_annotation_column)) {
  key_annotation_audit_note <- paste(
    "No key annotation column is configured.",
    "Set `key_annotation_column` in the YAML to a metadata column such as `disease_status`",
    "to count retained segments for the main comparison."
  )
  key_annotation_audit_available <- FALSE
} else if (is.na(key_annotation_col)) {
  key_annotation_audit_note <- paste(
    "The configured key annotation column was not found after QC filtering:",
    key_annotation_column,
    "Update `key_annotation_column` to one of the available metadata columns listed in the warning."
  )
  key_annotation_audit_available <- FALSE
} else {
  key_annotation_audit_note <- ""
  key_annotation_audit_available <- TRUE
}

if (isTRUE(key_annotation_audit_available)) {
  key_annotation_segment_counts <- final_segment_metadata %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      Key_annotation = as.character(.data[[key_annotation_col]]),
      Segment = as.character(.data[[segment_audit_col]])
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Key_annotation = dplyr::if_else(
        is.na(.data$Key_annotation) | !nzchar(trimws(.data$Key_annotation)),
        "Missing annotation",
        trimws(.data$Key_annotation)
      ),
      Segment = dplyr::if_else(
        is.na(.data$Segment) | !nzchar(trimws(.data$Segment)),
        "Missing segment",
        trimws(.data$Segment)
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::count(Key_annotation, Segment, name = "Retained_segments") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Review_threshold = key_annotation_min_segments,
      Meets_review_threshold = .data$Retained_segments >= .data$Review_threshold
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(Key_annotation, Segment)

  readr::write_csv(
    key_annotation_segment_counts,
    fs::path(out_tables, "retained_segments_by_key_annotation_after_gene_loq.csv")
  )

  key_annotation_segment_counts_display <- key_annotation_segment_counts %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Meets_review_threshold = dplyr::if_else(.data$Meets_review_threshold, "Yes", "Review")
    ) %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      `Key annotation` = Key_annotation,
      `Segment type` = Segment,
      `Retained segments` = Retained_segments,
      `Review threshold` = Review_threshold,
      `Meets review threshold` = Meets_review_threshold
    )

  plot_segment_values <- sort(unique(key_annotation_segment_counts$Segment))
  plot_segment_palette <- segment_palette[intersect(plot_segment_values, names(segment_palette))]
  missing_segment_values <- setdiff(plot_segment_values, names(plot_segment_palette))
  if (length(missing_segment_values) > 0) {
    extra_segment_colors <- grDevices::colorRampPalette(
      protocol_categorical_colours()
    )(length(missing_segment_values))
    plot_segment_palette <- c(
      plot_segment_palette,
      stats::setNames(extra_segment_colors, missing_segment_values)
    )
  }
  plot_segment_palette <- plot_segment_palette[plot_segment_values]

  key_annotation_segment_plot <- ggplot(
    key_annotation_segment_counts,
    aes(x = .data$Key_annotation, y = .data$Retained_segments, fill = .data$Segment)
  ) +
    geom_col(
      position = position_dodge2(width = 0.8, preserve = "single"),
      width = 0.72,
      color = "grey30",
      linewidth = 0.2
    ) +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = key_annotation_min_segments,
      linetype = "dashed",
      color = "#8B1E3F",
      linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = .data$Retained_segments),
      position = position_dodge2(width = 0.8, preserve = "single"),
      vjust = -0.35,
      size = 3
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(),
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = plot_segment_palette, drop = FALSE) +
    theme_bw(base_family = protocol_font_family) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "right"
    ) +
    labs(
      x = key_annotation_col,
      y = "Retained segments after gene LOQ filtering",
      fill = "Segment type"
    )

  if (isTRUE(params$save$save_plots)) {
    save_plot(
      filename = fs::path(out_figures, "retained_segments_by_key_annotation_after_gene_loq.png"),
      plot = key_annotation_segment_plot,
      base_height = 4.8,
      base_width = min(12, max(6, 1.2 * dplyr::n_distinct(key_annotation_segment_counts$Key_annotation))),
      bg = "white"
    )
  }
} else {
  key_annotation_segment_counts_display <- tibble::tibble(
    Result = key_annotation_audit_note
  )

  key_annotation_segment_plot <- ggplot() +
    annotate(
      "text",
      x = 0,
      y = 0,
      label = stringr::str_wrap(key_annotation_audit_note, width = 75),
      lineheight = 1.15,
      size = 3.5,
      color = "#333333"
    ) +
    xlim(-1, 1) +
    ylim(-1, 1) +
    theme_void(base_family = protocol_font_family) +
    labs(caption = "Set `key_annotation_column` in the YAML to enable this retained-segment audit.")
}
```

```{r show-key-annotation-retained-segment-table}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

render_protocol_table(
  key_annotation_segment_counts_display,
  caption = "Retained segment counts after gene LOQ filtering by configured key annotation and segment type."
)
```

```{r fig-keyAnnotationRetainedSegments}
#| fig-cap: Retained segment counts after gene LOQ filtering by the configured key annotation and segment type. The dashed line marks the review threshold for each annotation/segment group.
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5
#| message: false
#| warning: false

key_annotation_segment_plot
```

::: {.callout-important}
## What to check

- Expected pattern: each group needed for the intended comparison has enough retained segments in the segment types you plan to analyze.
- Concerning pattern: one disease, treatment, or condition group has only a few retained segments in a required segment type.
- Next decision: if important groups fall below the review threshold, record the issue and decide whether to revise QC thresholds, combine groups, remove an underpowered contrast, or collect more data before differential analysis.
:::

After QC filtering, the feature and segment (sample) numbers retained, compared with
the counts entering detection filtering, are summarised below. A large drop is
flagged here so it is not missed before later stages.

```{r final-qc-retained-summary}
#| message: false

qc_features_end <- nrow(target_spatial_data)
qc_segments_end <- ncol(target_spatial_data)

pct_retained <- function(end, start) {
  if (is.null(start) || length(start) == 0 || is.na(start) || start <= 0) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  end / start
}

qc_retained_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  Measure = c("Features (genes)", "Segments (AOIs)"),
  `Entering detection filtering` = c(qc_features_start, qc_segments_start),
  `Retained after QC` = c(qc_features_end, qc_segments_end),
  Removed = c(
    qc_features_start - qc_features_end,
    qc_segments_start - qc_segments_end
  ),
  `Percent retained` = scales::percent(
    c(
      pct_retained(qc_features_end, qc_features_start),
      pct_retained(qc_segments_end, qc_segments_start)
    ),
    accuracy = 1
  )
)

render_protocol_table(
  qc_retained_summary,
  row.names = FALSE,
  caption = "Feature and segment counts entering detection filtering versus retained after QC."
)

# Surface a single, consolidated warning if QC removed a large fraction overall.
feature_retained_fraction <- pct_retained(qc_features_end, qc_features_start)
segment_retained_fraction <- pct_retained(qc_segments_end, qc_segments_start)
if (!is.na(segment_retained_fraction) && segment_retained_fraction <= 0.5) {
  warning(sprintf(
    "QC retained only %s of segments (%d of %d). Confirm enough segments remain for the planned comparison before continuing.",
    scales::percent(segment_retained_fraction, accuracy = 1),
    qc_segments_end, qc_segments_start
  ), call. = FALSE)
}
if (!is.na(feature_retained_fraction) && feature_retained_fraction <= 0.5) {
  warning(sprintf(
    "QC retained only %s of features (%d of %d). Confirm enough genes remain for normalization and modelling before continuing.",
    scales::percent(feature_retained_fraction, accuracy = 1),
    qc_features_end, qc_features_start
  ), call. = FALSE)
}
```

::: {.callout-important}
## Check how much data QC removed
Read the retention table above before moving on. A low **Percent retained** for
segments is common on lower-depth datasets, where fewer genes clear the per-segment
detection cutoff, so most segments are removed. If too few segments or features
remain for your planned comparison, relax the detection thresholds
(`qc$min_gene_detection_rate_per_segment` and
`qc$min_gene_detection_rate_across_segments`) and re-render before continuing,
because later stages such as PCA and differential expression need enough
samples and genes to run.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Session Info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
:::
